name: neon

env_axis.name: ecoclimatic-domain
env_axis.shift_mechanism: 20 ecoclimatic Domains; cross-Domain hold-out tests cross-ecology transfer with standardized protocols

ood_protocol.name: neon-cross-domain
ood_protocol.ref: Keller et al. 2008 BioScience (NEON design); Read & Hamilton 2017

facts: 81 field sites across 20 ecoclimatic Domains; standardized 30-year longitudinal collection (NSF, ~10 years collected so far).
hypotheses: Cross-Domain hold-out is a concept shift rather than a covariate shift: different Domains exhibit categorically different ecosystem dynamics, not just the same ecology under different climate zones.
Standardized protocols do not fully eliminate measurement-as-confounder: site-specific factors (technician, equipment age) leak through despite standardization.
intuitions: NEON's own cross-Domain analyses likely exist in the published literature.
FLUXNET-NEON comparisons probably exist, drawing on the smaller-scale earlier eddy-covariance network.
Ecological-foundation-model attempts using NEON likely appeared in 2024-25.
unknowns: The 20 ecoclimatic Domains are NEON's curatorial classification; whether this captures the natural ecological partition or imposes one cannot be determined from NEON alone.
Live collection with non-uniform site-start dates means year-as-env may not be a coherent axis, in ways not exposed by the metadata.
epistemic_state.boundary: USA-only (CONUS+AK+PR); no global cross-continent shift accessible. H=3 (richest hierarchy in corpus) but no compositional handle within individuals.

representational_commitments: Standardized protocols across sites (protocol choice itself is implicit)
materialization_status: HARV (D01) soil-water-content product DP1.00094.001 for 2022-01 (63 MB, ~30 CSV files). Other Domains/sites/products deferred — same API pattern works (data.neonscience.org/api/v0/data/<product>/<site>/<month>).
